teaching_language: English (study track wording appears on some official pages) [uncertain]

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent in geological sciences or a related field, with preparation in geosciences, planetary sciences, and remote sensing exploration.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Geological Science and Technologies, or related subjects and/or proven skills in geosciences, planetary sciences and remote sensing exploration.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Applicants must verify the University of Padua's country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements. No separate programme-wide GPA threshold is published on the reviewed official 2026/2027 admissions pages for this programme.

accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum score 126.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if they obtained the entry qualification by completing their previous education entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. Applicants can apply for the degree programme only once per Academic Year.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not available for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. Applicants can apply for the degree programme only once per Academic Year.

required_documents.notes: Upload a colour scanned PDF. If needed, provide an English or Italian translation. No legalised documents are required at application stage.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification or provisional enrolment certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.
The University asks applicants to check the entry requirements of the chosen programme for any additional required document.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment; a CIMEA Statement of Verification can be used as an alternative in the cases stated by the University.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The transcript must be issued by the competent academic authority and show passed exams, grades, and programme description.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and study visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly no later than 15 July and obtain a study visa.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants who are in Italy at enrolment stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No separate programme-specific entry exam stated
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The reviewed official admissions pages describe admission through verification of curricular and language requirements, but do not publish a separate written test or interview for all applicants.

source_quotes.quote: Access Open access with admission requirements

A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Geological Science and Technologies, or related subjects and/or proven skills in geosciences, planetary sciences and remote sensing exploration.
Call Three: 2 June 2026 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)
Planetary Geosciences - study track held in English of the Master's degree programme Geologia Ambientale e Dinamica della Terra. New programme 2026/2027 (to be approved)
Geoscienze per la terra, l'ambiente e i pianeti – curricula "Earth Dynamics" e "Planetary Geosciences"

uncertainty_notes: Official University sources conflict on the programme's status. The English degrees page describes Planetary Geosciences as an English-taught study track of the master's degree programme 'Geologia Ambientale e Dinamica della Terra' and labels it 'New programme 2026/2027 (to be approved)', while apply.unipd.it and the educational-offer page present Planetary Geosciences as a master's degree with its own admissions entry.
Because of this conflict, the teaching-language wording is marked uncertain and should be rechecked against any final approved 2026/2027 admission notice if a dedicated PDF call is later published.
